lenses.guidance: vv 1-12's perimeter description — south, west, north, east — and vv 13-29's tribal allotters form a two-part literary structure. Boundaries first, then assignments. The chapter's form treats the land as a defined gift rather than open territory.

vv 1-12's land boundaries echo throughout Scripture into Ezek 47:13-20's eschatological land allotment, where the same cardinal-direction structure recurs. The canonical map is not abandoned at exile but extended at the prophetic horizon.
